question_title: Will any UEFA Champions League 2025–26 quarter-final first leg end 0–0?
summary: The question asks whether any of the 4 UCL 2025-26 quarter-final first-leg matches will end 0-0. Historical data from 2009-10 through 2024-25 (15 seasons, 60 QF first-leg matches) shows exactly 3 instances of 0-0 results: AC Milan 0-0 Barcelona (2011-12), Malaga 0-0 Dortmund (2012-13), and Atletico 0-0 Real Madrid (2014-15). The season-level base rate is 3/15 = 20%. However, there has been a 9-season drought with 0 out of 36 matches ending 0-0 since 2014-15. This drought has ~15% probability under the no-change hypothesis, providing mild (not strong) evidence of a structural shift. Bayesian mixture models weighting both the full historical sample and recent-only data yield estimates of 14-17% for P(at least one 0-0 in 4 matches). The QF matchups are still unknown (R16 begins March 10, 2026), adding some uncertainty. My estimate of 18% appropriately balances the 20% season-level base rate with mild downward pressure from the recent drought.

factors.description: Historical season-level base rate: 3 out of 15 seasons (20%) had at least one 0-0 QF first-leg result. This is the most directly relevant statistic.
Recent 9-season drought: 0 out of 36 QF first legs ended 0-0 since 2014-15. P(drought | no structural change) ≈ 15%, providing mild evidence of lower rate.
QF teams are elite attacking sides (top 8 in Europe), making scoreless draws inherently less likely than in general football where ~8% of matches end 0-0.
Away goals rule abolished since 2021-22 could theoretically encourage more cautious first legs, but no observable effect in 4 post-abolition seasons (0/16).
4 independent matches provide multiple chances for a 0-0 - the question asks about ANY match, which roughly quadruples the per-match probability.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 18% probability (logit: -1.5)**

Key reasoning:

- **Historical base rate**: 3 out of 15 UCL seasons (2009-2025) had at least one 0-0 QF first leg = 20% season-level rate
- **Per-match rate**: 3 out of 60 QF first-leg matches (5.0%) ended 0-0
- **The three 0-0 results**: AC Milan 0-0 Barcelona (2012), Malaga 0-0 Dortmund (2013), Atletico 0-0 Real Madrid (2015)
- **Recent drought**: 0 out of 36 QF first legs in the last 9 seasons - but P(drought | no change) ≈ 15%, so this is only mild evidence of structural change
- **Tournament status**: R16 hasn't been played yet (starts March 10), so QF matchups are unknown
- **Bayesian analysis**: Full-sample Bayesian integration gives 23%, recent-only gives 10%, mixture models center around 16-17%

The 18% estimate reflects the 20% historical base rate with a small discount for the recent (but not statistically compelling) drought in 0-0 results.
